name: Palak Paneer

hero.alt: Finished palak paneer in a black kadhai with paneer cubes and cream swirls
hero.caption: palak paneer — vibrant green gravy, paneer cubes

healthFacts: Spinach contains non-heme iron — vitamin C from tomatoes and lemon doubles its absorption.
Paneer completes the amino acid profile missing in spinach — together they're a near-complete protein.
Blanching and shocking spinach preserves chlorophyll and slows oxalate formation.
Adding cream off-heat retains more fat-soluble vitamins (A, K, E) than cooking them in.
Ginger in the masala enhances thermogenesis and reduces spinach's natural bitterness.
One serving provides ~60% of daily vitamin K needs — essential for bone density.

steps.title: Blanch the spinach
steps.detail: Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Add spinach and push it under — blanch for exactly 2 minutes. Transfer immediately to a bowl of ice water. This shocks the spinach and locks in the vivid green color.
steps.tip: The ice bath is not optional — without it, the spinach goes army-green and bitter-tasting.

media.alt: Blanched spinach cooling in ice water beside a steaming pot
media.caption: Blanch and shock

steps.title: Blend smooth
steps.detail: Drain the spinach. Squeeze out excess water gently. Blend with green chili and a splash of water until completely smooth. Set aside. The puree should be a vibrant, bright green.
steps.tip: Over-blending with too much water makes the gravy thin and watery. Use as little as needed.

media.alt: Smooth bright green spinach puree in a blender jar with green chilies nearby
media.caption: Vibrant spinach puree

steps.title: Build the base
steps.detail: Heat butter in a pan. Splutter cumin seeds. Add finely chopped onion and cook 5 minutes until light golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 minutes. Add chopped tomato and Kashmiri chili. Cook until tomato breaks down completely.
steps.tip: Don't skip the tomato — it adds acidity that brightens the spinach and boosts iron absorption.

media.alt: Onion, tomato, ginger-garlic paste, cumin, and Kashmiri chili cooking in butter
media.caption: Tomato-onion masala

steps.title: Add spinach puree
steps.detail: Pour the spinach puree into the pan. Stir to combine with the masala. Add salt and ¼ cup water if the gravy is too thick. Cook on medium for 5–6 minutes, stirring frequently.
steps.tip: The gravy will darken slightly as it cooks — that's fine. Don't overcook or it loses vibrancy.

media.alt: Bright spinach puree stirred into red tomato masala in a black kadhai
media.caption: Spinach puree simmer

steps.title: Add paneer
steps.detail: Cut paneer into 1-inch cubes. For softer paneer, soak the cubes in warm water for 10 minutes before adding. Slide them into the gravy gently. Stir once or twice — don't be aggressive.
steps.tip: Paneer doesn't need to 'cook' — it just needs to warm through and absorb the gravy.

media.alt: Paneer cubes nestled into thick green palak gravy before cream is added
media.caption: Paneer warmed through

steps.title: Finish and serve
steps.detail: Add garam masala and cream. Stir gently. Squeeze in a few drops of lemon juice — it brightens everything. Taste and adjust salt. Serve immediately with roti, naan, or rice.
steps.tip: Add cream off-heat or swirl it in last for richness without losing the green color.

media.alt: Palak paneer finished with cream, lemon, and paneer cubes in a black kadhai
media.caption: Creamy palak finish
